evals.prompt: We run a chain of retail stores across four regions and I need a dashboard for our weekly leadership meeting. The main questions we want to answer are: how is overall revenue trending month over month, which regions are pulling their weight and which are falling behind, and which product categories are driving growth versus dragging us down. We also want to quickly see the headline numbers — total revenue, total units moved, and average order value — so the execs can get the picture in the first five seconds. It would also be really useful to have a heatmap showing revenue by region and month so we can spot seasonal patterns at a glance — like which regions spike during the holidays and which ones dip in summer. The audience is regional directors who are not technical but need to make staffing and inventory decisions based on what they see. Generate some realistic-looking mock data for this.
evals.expected_output: A working Vizro dashboard that launches without errors, with pages addressing the business questions, KPIs, regional/product breakdowns, and a region-by-month heatmap.
expectations: All 5 spec files (spec/1 through spec/5) are generated
Agent tested the dashboard in a browser (using any automation tool — e.g., Playwright), fixed console errors and callback errors on page navigation, and produced a bug-free app.py
Agent ran validate_colors.py and produced a PASS result (no hardcoded colors in app.py)
Grid layout uses 12 columns with rectangular components
Interactive options were presented during the design phase
app.py uses vizro.plotly.express (not plain plotly.express)
Agent ran validate_aggregation.py and produced a PASS result (bar/line charts use @capture('graph') with pre-aggregated data)
KPI cards use built-in kpi_card or kpi_card_reference (not rebuilt as custom charts)
Heatmap uses Vizro default color palette (no explicit color_continuous_scale like 'Blues' or 'RdYlGn')
spec/2_interaction_ux.yaml either contains an `interactions:` block mapping each interaction to a named pattern from wiring-vizro-actions, or explicitly justifies why standard filters suffice
If cross-filter/cross-highlight is implemented, it uses `va.set_control` (NOT the deprecated `filter_interaction` API)
Any interactive source component (Graph or AgGrid with `actions=`) has a short, action-oriented `header` hint (e.g. 'Click a bar to filter by region')

If a custom `@capture('graph')` function is used as a cross-filter source (its `vm.Graph` has `actions=va.set_control(...)`), the function signature accepts `custom_data` as a parameter (e.g. `def my_chart(data_frame, custom_data, **kwargs)`) — hardcoding `custom_data=...` inside the function body causes a callback 500 when the action fires
Agent clicked on every interactive source (any Graph/AgGrid with `actions=`) during browser testing and confirmed no callback errors / no 500 on `_dash-update-component`

evals.prompt: I work at a SaaS company and our product team needs a way to monitor feature adoption. We want to understand which features users are actually engaging with after launch, how adoption rates change over time, and whether there is a difference between free-tier and paid-tier users. The PM wants to see at a glance how many users tried each feature in the last 30 days, what the retention curve looks like for each feature, and which features have high trial rates but low stickiness — because those are the ones where the UX probably needs work. This is for the weekly product review so it needs to be clear enough for non-technical stakeholders. Please generate mock data that makes sense for this.
evals.expected_output: A working dashboard with feature adoption metrics, tier comparisons, and retention insights.
expectations: All 5 spec files are generated
Agent tested the dashboard in a browser (using any automation tool — e.g., Playwright), fixed console errors and callback errors on page navigation, and produced a bug-free app.py
Agent ran validate_colors.py and produced a PASS result (no hardcoded colors in app.py)
Grid layout uses 12 columns with rectangular components
KPI cards use built-in kpi_card or kpi_card_reference (not rebuilt as custom charts)
app.py uses vizro.plotly.express (not plain plotly.express)
Agent ran validate_aggregation.py and produced a PASS result (bar/line charts use @capture('graph') with pre-aggregated data)
Interactive options were presented during the design phase
spec/2_interaction_ux.yaml either contains an `interactions:` block mapping each interaction to a named pattern from wiring-vizro-actions, or explicitly justifies why standard filters suffice

If cross-highlight is implemented (e.g. click a feature to highlight in retention chart), the custom chart accepts a `highlight_X` argument and the Parameter has `visible=False` with `'NONE'` in selector options
If any `actions=` is used, no deprecated `filter_interaction` calls appear in app.py

If a custom `@capture('graph')` function is used as a cross-filter source (its `vm.Graph` has `actions=va.set_control(...)`), the function signature accepts `custom_data` as a parameter — hardcoding `custom_data=...` inside the function body causes a callback 500 when the action fires
Agent clicked on every interactive source (any Graph/AgGrid with `actions=`) during browser testing and confirmed no callback errors / no 500 on `_dash-update-component`

evals.prompt: We manage a fleet of 200 delivery trucks and the ops team needs a dashboard to monitor daily performance. The key questions are: how many deliveries are we completing versus planned, what is the on-time delivery rate by depot and by driver, and where are the bottlenecks — is it loading time, transit time, or last-mile delays? We also want to flag trucks that are consistently underperforming or routes that always run late so we can investigate whether it is a vehicle issue, a driver issue, or a route planning issue. The audience is the dispatch manager and the regional ops leads. They want to be able to drill into the raw delivery records when something looks off. Generate mock data for about 6 months of operations across 5 depots.
evals.expected_output: A working multi-page dashboard with fleet KPIs, depot/driver breakdowns, and a detailed data table for drill-down.
expectations: All 5 spec files are generated
Agent tested the dashboard in a browser (using any automation tool — e.g., Playwright), fixed console errors and callback errors on page navigation, and produced a bug-free app.py
Agent ran validate_colors.py and produced a PASS result (no hardcoded colors in app.py)
Grid layout uses 12 columns with rectangular components
AG Grid is used for the detailed data table
Interactive options were presented during the design phase
app.py uses vizro.plotly.express (not plain plotly.express)
Agent ran validate_aggregation.py and produced a PASS result (bar/line charts use @capture('graph') with pre-aggregated data)
KPI cards use built-in kpi_card or kpi_card_reference (not rebuilt as custom charts)
spec/2_interaction_ux.yaml contains an `interactions:` block — drilling into raw delivery records is a natural fit for Pattern 1 (Hierarchical Drill-Down) or Pattern 2 (Single-Page Drill-Down)

If cross-page drill-through is implemented: the target page Filter has `show_in_url=True`, the target page includes a back `vm.Button(href=...)` as the first component, and the target page uses `layout=vm.Flex(direction='column')`
If cross-filter is implemented within a page: `va.set_control` references an explicit Filter `id`, and the source component has a `header` hint
No deprecated `filter_interaction` calls in app.py

If a custom `@capture('graph')` function is used as a cross-filter source (its `vm.Graph` has `actions=va.set_control(...)`), the function signature accepts `custom_data` as a parameter — hardcoding `custom_data=...` inside the function body causes a callback 500 when the action fires
Agent clicked on every interactive source (any Graph/AgGrid or row with `actions=`) during browser testing and confirmed no callback errors / no 500 on `_dash-update-component`
